title: CatalystEvent
description: A discrete, timestamped macro event that may drive sector repricing. Decays in relevance over time.

geography.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 or region codes (EU, GLOBAL, APAC, EM)

novelty_score.description: Rubric-derived. Sum of 5 binary questions × 20 each. See scoring_weights.yaml novelty_score_rubric. Never assign directly — compute from novelty_rubric_scores below.

novelty_rubric_scores.description: Answers to the 5 novelty rubric questions (yes=true, no=false). novelty_score = count(true) × 20.

strength_score.description: Composite: magnitude × surprise × confirmation. Main sort key. Unlike novelty_score, this can be continuous — it reflects event impact, not originality.

consensus_surprise.description: 0=fully expected, 1=complete surprise. Use 0.1 increments only — avoids false precision.

is_priced_in_estimate.description: Stepped estimate of how much is already in price. Use one of 5 levels only. See scoring_weights.yaml is_priced_in_levels for anchored criteria. Never use intermediate values.

relation_to_structural.description: Relationship of this event to its linked structural catalyst. Determines scoring interaction: confirms → amplification, contradicts → dampening, independent → additive. Required when related_catalyst_ids contains a struct_ ID. See scoring_weights.yaml catalyst_interaction_factors.

decay_halflife_days.description: Days for relevance to decay to 50%. Default 46 (lambda=0.015). Adjust for policy catalysts (longer) vs data releases (shorter).

related_catalyst_ids.description: IDs of related catalysts (struct_ or cat_). If a struct_ ID is included, relation_to_structural must be set.

user_rank.description: User-assigned priority. 1=highest. null=unranked (treated as rank 3, multiplier 1.0).
